product.name: Starlight
product.definition: A unified platform to run virtual machines, containers, and AI workloads directly at the edge — with security and autonomy embedded into the system itself.

pillars.name: Unified
pillars.claim: VMs, containers, AI — one control plane.

why.headline: Built for scale. Not for constraint.
why.thesis: Two decades of datacenter infrastructure assumed reliable networks, abundant compute, and dedicated teams to operate complexity at scale. The world has moved to the edge, where those assumptions break down.

waves.title: Consolidation
waves.detail: Workloads consolidated in centralized environments. Assumed reliable networks and abundant compute.

waves.title: Orchestration
waves.detail: Containers & Kubernetes scaled fleets to thousands of machines — built for constant coordination, not constraint.

waves.title: Modern AI
waves.detail: Inference moves close to users and data — where centralized assumptions about network, scale, and trust break down.

unified_by_design.headline: One API. Every workload.
unified_by_design.summary: VMs, containers, and AI managed through one operational model. Desktop, CLI, or automation — all pointing at the same control plane.

shared.name: Shared identity
shared.detail: Same auth, same roles, across every workload type.

shared.name: Shared policy
shared.detail: One enforcement model — VMs, containers, and AI alike.

shared.name: Shared lifecycle
shared.detail: Deploy, observe, and retire with one set of primitives.

vmware_takeout.headline: Off VMware. On a modern platform.
vmware_takeout.summary: Replace the VMware stack with a platform that runs the same VMs — plus containers and AI — under pricing you can predict and a license you can understand.

compare.vmware: Forced bundles; features you don't use
compare.starlight: One SKU. What you run is what you pay for
VMs, containers, and AI on one platform

compatibility.name: Workload compatible
compatibility.detail: Existing VMs — Windows and Linux — run on Starlight. Bring images as-is.

compatibility.name: Feature compatible
compatibility.detail: Live migration, HA, snapshots, networking — the feature set teams rely on, natively.

compatibility.name: Move at your pace
compatibility.detail: Run both during transition. Consolidate when you're ready — no forklift required.

containers.headline: Containers. Without Kubernetes complexity.
containers.model: An entirely new orchestration model. Every node is autonomous — it tracks its own resources and shares state peer-to-peer with the cluster. No brittle central control plane.

kubernetes.detail: Every node depends on the control plane.
kubernetes.failure: If the control plane is unreachable, the cluster drifts.

starlight.detail: Every node is autonomous; state shared by protocol.
starlight.behavior: Each node runs on its own. The cluster stays consistent.

benefits.name: No control plane to maintain
benefits.detail: No etcd, no scheduler, no operator sprawl. Less to run, less to break.

benefits.name: Runs disconnected
benefits.detail: Nodes operate independently when the network drops. State reconverges on its own.

benefits.name: Same artifacts
benefits.detail: Existing OCI images run as-is. Helm charts and K8s workloads replatform incrementally.

ai.headline: Everything you need to run AI.
ai.summary: Models, drivers, protocols, and agent control — shipped as part of the platform. No separate AI stack. No glue work. Deploy inference the same way you deploy any workload.

capabilities.title: Ship AI like you ship apps.
capabilities.detail: Models are delivered as OCI container images. Same packaging, same lifecycle, same primitives as every other workload — from dev to edge.

capabilities.title: Ready on first boot.
capabilities.detail: GPU drivers, accelerator runtimes, and inference toolchains preconfigured and version-matched. No driver hell, no kernel-module roulette.

capabilities.title: Agents speak natively.
capabilities.detail: A built-in Model Context Protocol server exposes Starlight to AI agents, so tools and models can interact with the platform over a standard interface.

capabilities.title: AI operates Starlight.
capabilities.detail: Use agents to deploy, observe, and manage infrastructure — not just workloads. Control plane is programmable end-to-end.

ai_security.principle: Models are untrusted code. Starlight enforces that.
controls: Data paths & egress controlled by policy

edge.headline: Autonomy, not central control.
edge.summary: Each location operates on its own. Workloads keep running during disconnection. Systems synchronize state when connectivity returns.

stats.name: High availability
stats.detail: First-class HA without a three-node cluster. Matches edge reality — not datacenter baseline.

stats.name: Disconnected operation
stats.detail: Nodes function independently when the network drops. No dependency on a central system to keep workloads alive.

stats.name: Platform, every scale
stats.detail: Single node to distributed fleet — same operating model. Start small and expand without rework.

security.headline: Security is part of the architecture.
security.summary: Integrated identity, policy enforcement, a hardened OS foundation, and runtime protection for every workload — including AI.

adoption.headline: Bring your workloads with you.
adoption.summary: Most teams aren't starting from scratch. Starlight is a new operating model — not a rewrite of the one you already run.
path: Helm charts adapt without rewriting apps
CI/CD keeps delivering the same artifacts
adoption.positioning: Starlight is built by Mainsail Industries for organizations operating where infrastructure must function with limited resources, unreliable networks, and no room for failure.

glossary.MCP: Model Context Protocol — interface that lets AI agents drive Starlight directly
glossary.OCI: Open Container Initiative — container image standard
glossary.STIG: Security Technical Implementation Guide — DoD security configuration standard
glossary.Two-node HA: High availability with only two nodes — first-class target for edge sites
